description: Traceability metadata mapping Phase 3 OPA policies to Phase 2 Lambda remediation functions. This file documents the feedback loop — runtime remediation patterns informing commit-time prevention policies.

policies.title: IAM Wildcard Permission Prevention

phase2_source.runtime_action: Removes wildcard statements from IAM policy documents

policies.phase3_action: Blocks PR when Terraform IAM policy contains wildcard Action on wildcard Resource
deny_rules: IAM role trust policy with Principal '*'
policies.traceability_proof: The dangerous_actions set in iam.rego is identical to dangerous_patterns list in iam_remediation.py (L141-145)

policies.title: S3 Public Access & Encryption Prevention

phase2_source.runtime_action: Enables all 4 public access blocks, SSE-S3 encryption, and versioning

policies.phase3_action: Blocks PR when S3 bucket lacks public access block, encryption, or has any public access setting disabled
deny_rules: Any of the 4 public access block settings set to false
S3 bucket without server-side encryption configuration
policies.traceability_proof: The 4 public access block checks in s3.rego (block_public_acls, ignore_public_acls, block_public_policy, restrict_public_buckets) map 1:1 to the public_access_block_config dict in s3_remediation.py (L238-243)

policies.title: Security Group Open Access Prevention

phase2_source.runtime_action: Revokes ingress rules allowing 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 on dangerous ports

policies.phase3_action: Blocks PR when security group allows ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 on any of 11 dangerous ports
deny_rules: Standalone security group rules with dangerous ports
warn_rules: Wide port range (>100 ports) open to public
policies.traceability_proof: The dangerous_ports set in sg.rego contains the same 11 ports as the dangerous_ports set in sg_remediation.py (L214-226)

policies.title: Cross-Service Encryption Enforcement

phase2_source.runtime_action: Enables SSE-S3 encryption on S3 buckets; extended to other services in Phase 3

policies.phase3_action: Blocks PR when DynamoDB, SQS, or SNS resources lack encryption configuration
deny_rules: SQS queue without SSE-SQS or KMS encryption
warn_rules: CloudWatch Log Group without KMS encryption
policies.traceability_proof: Extends the Phase 2 S3 encryption enforcement pattern to all services used by IaC-Secure-Gate

policies.title: Required Resource Tags

phase2_source.description: Cross-cutting concern derived from project infrastructure patterns
phase2_source.runtime_action: N/A — tagging is a preventive-only control

policies.phase3_action: Blocks PR when taggable resources are missing Project, Environment, or ManagedBy tags

required_tags.Project: Identifies IaC-Secure-Gate resources for cost tracking
required_tags.Environment: Distinguishes dev/staging/prod (valid values: dev, staging, prod)

deny_rules: Any taggable resource missing required tags
ManagedBy tag with value other than 'terraform'
policies.traceability_proof: Tags are used throughout Phase 1 and Phase 2 Terraform modules for resource identification and are now enforced as a commit-time requirement

policies.title: Budget-Aware Constraints

phase2_source.description: Derived from the project's monthly budget constraint and Phase 2 infrastructure decisions
phase2_source.runtime_action: N/A — cost governance is a preventive-only control

policies.phase3_action: Blocks PR when configurations would exceed the project budget
policies.traceability_proof: Phase 2 DynamoDB uses PAY_PER_REQUEST and Lambda functions use 128-256MB memory — these limits are now enforced as commit-time constraints

feedback_loop_summary.description: The feedback loop is the key academic contribution of Phase 3. Runtime remediation patterns discovered in Phase 2 are translated into commit-time prevention policies in Phase 3. This creates a closed loop: violations that occur in production inform policies that prevent the same violations from being deployed again.
flow: Phase 1: CloudTrail + Config + Access Analyzer detect misconfiguration
Phase 2: EventBridge triggers Lambda to remediate automatically (MTTR: 1.66s)
Phase 2 insight: Lambda code contains the exact patterns that define 'dangerous'
Phase 3: Those same patterns are encoded as OPA/Rego deny rules
Phase 3 result: Violation is blocked at PR time (MTTP: 0s — never reaches AWS)
